content:
# Hebrews Chapter 10 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-06

---

### v.1 — "a shadow of the good things to come" vs. "a dim preview of the good things to come"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "having a shadow of the good things to come, not the very image of the things" | skian gar echōn ho nomos tōn mellontōn agathōn, ouk autēn tēn eikona tōn pragmatōn |
| LOGOS | "the law has but a shadow of the good things to come instead of the true form of these realities" | "True form" for eikona |
| PNEUMA | "only a shadow, a dim preview of the good things to come, not the good things themselves" | Adds "dim"; "the good things themselves" for eikona tōn pragmatōn |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "dim" — the author says "shadow" (skia) without qualifying it. More significantly, PNEUMA renders eikona tōn pragmatōn ("the image/form of the realities") as "the good things themselves" — collapsing the distinction between "image" and "reality." The author distinguishes three levels: shadow (law), image (Christ's sacrifice), realities (heavenly things).

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA collapses the shadow/image/reality distinction.

---

### v.19 — "confidence to enter the holy places" vs. "boldly enter heaven's Most Holy Place"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "boldness for the entrance into the holy places by the blood of Yeshua" | parrēsian eis tēn eisodon tōn hagiōn |
| LOGOS | "confidence to enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "we can boldly enter heaven's Most Holy Place because of the blood of Jesus" | Adds "heaven's"; specifies "Most Holy Place" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. The author says "the holy places" (tōn hagiōn) — plural, general. PNEUMA specifies "heaven's Most Holy Place" — adding both the location (heaven) and the specification (Most Holy Place). The author's language is deliberately broad: through Christ's blood, we enter the holy places — the entire sanctuary, not just the inner chamber.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA over-specifies.

---

### v.22 — "hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience" vs. "our guilty consciences have been sprinkled with Christ's blood"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience" | rerantismenoi tas kardias apo syneidēseōs ponēras |
| LOGOS | "with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "our guilty consciences have been sprinkled with Christ's blood to make us clean" | Adds "with Christ's blood"; "guilty" for ponēras; restructures |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. The author says the hearts are "sprinkled from an evil conscience" — the sprinkling removes the evil conscience. PNEUMA adds "with Christ's blood" (not stated here, though implied from v.19) and restructures the sentence. The author's language is compressed and allusive — the sprinkling echoes the Day of Atonement ritual without naming its agent.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds agent and restructures.

---

### v.29 — "outraged the Spirit of grace" vs. "insulted and disdained the Holy Spirit who brings God's mercy to us"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "insulted the Spirit of grace" | to pneuma tēs charitos enybrisas |
| LOGOS | "outraged the Spirit of grace" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "insulted and disdained the Holy Spirit who brings God's mercy to us" | Doubles to "insulted and disdained"; adds "who brings God's mercy to us" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. enybrizō means "to insult/outrage" — one verb. PNEUMA doubles to "insulted and disdained." PNEUMA also expands "the Spirit of grace" to "the Holy Spirit who brings God's mercy to us" — adding a relative clause that interprets grace as mercy and adds a function not stated by the author.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA doubles the verb and expands.

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA over-specifies | 2 | "Heaven's Most Holy Place" (v.19), "with Christ's blood" (v.22) |
| PNEUMA expands/doubles | 1 | "Insulted and disdained" + relative clause (v.29) |
| PNEUMA collapses distinctions | 1 | Shadow/image/reality → shadow/reality (v.1) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 10's most significant disagreement is v.1 — PNEUMA collapses the author's three-tier metaphysics (shadow → image → reality) into a two-tier system (shadow → reality). The author distinguishes between the law's shadow (skia), Christ's image/form (eikōn), and the heavenly realities (pragmata). PNEUMA's "not the good things themselves" merges image and reality, losing the middle term that the author carefully preserves.

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
